perturbation.original_text: (b) "Endorsed Product" shall mean a putter manufactured, distributed, promoted, advertised and sold by TPC bearing the "Teardrop Putter" name and/or logo.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) "Endorsed Product" shall mean a product manufactured, distributed, promoted, advertised and sold by TPC.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific requirement that the endorsed product bears the "Teardrop Putter" name and/or logo, the contract now allows for ambiguity. This contradicts Section 2, which states that Consolidated Artists agrees to cause Ogle to use the Endorsed Product during the Contract Period and throughout the Contract Territory whenever he is playing competitive golf or otherwise participating in golf clinics and outings, since the section does not specify which product will be endorsed, and leaves it ambigious of what is the Endorsed Product.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. OGLE TO USE ENDORSED PRODUCT. Consolidated Artists agrees to cause Ogle to use the Endorsed Product during the Contract Period and throughout the Contract Territory whenever he is playing competitive golf or otherwise participating in golf clinics and outings.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines the "Endorsed Product" as a putter with the "Teardrop Putter" name and/or logo. The changed text removes this specific requirement, making the definition ambiguous. This directly contradicts Section 2, which requires Ogle to use the "Endorsed Product". Without the specific definition, it becomes unclear what product Ogle is obligated to use, creating a strong in-text contradiction and potential unenforceability because the endorsed product is no longer clearly defined.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of Paragraphs 2, 3 and 4 above, TPC agrees that Consolidated Artists shall retain all rights in and to the Ogle Endorsement and shall not be prevented from using or permitting or licensing others to use his name or endorsement in connection with the promotion, advertisement, or sale of any product or service other than a golf putter in the Contract Territory during the Contract Period.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the provisions of Paragraphs 2 and 4 above, TPC agrees that Consolidated Artists shall retain all rights in and to the Ogle Endorsement and shall not be prevented from using or permitting or licensing others to use his name or endorsement in connection with the promotion, advertisement, or sale of any product or service other than a golf putter in the Contract Territory during the Contract Period.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Paragraph 3, the original agreement stated *GRANT OF ENDORSEMENT RIGHTS. Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, Consolidated Artists grants to TPC the exclusive right and license, within the Contract Territory and during the Contract Period, to use the Ogle Endorsement in connection with the manufacture, distribution, advertisement, promotion and sale of the Endorsed Product.* The altered text now creates a contradiction because Section 5 no longer recognizes the limitations imposed by the endorsement rights that were granted to TPC in Section 3. The omission creates in-text contradiction and uncertainty because while Section 3 grants TPC certain rights related to endorsements from Ogle, Section 5 fails to acknowledge such and appears to provide Consolidated Artists the ability to license Ogle's endorsement to competitors as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. GRANT OF ENDORSEMENT RIGHTS. Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, Consolidated Artists grants to TPC the exclusive right and license, within the Contract Territory and during the Contract Period, to use the Ogle Endorsement in connection with the manufacture, distribution, advertisement, promotion and sale of the Endorsed Product.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the reference to Paragraph 3, which grants TPC exclusive endorsement rights. This omission contradicts Paragraph 3 because Section 5, as altered, now allows Consolidated Artists to license Ogle's endorsement to others, conflicting with TPC's exclusive rights granted in Paragraph 3. This creates a clear in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: TPC agrees that it shall keep accurate and complete books and records showing all Endorsed Products manufactured, distributed and sold. Consolidated Artists and Advantage, at Consolidated Artists' expense, shall have the right during the Contract Period and until two (2) years after the termination of this Agreement to inspect and make copies of the books and records of TPC insofar as they relate to the computation of royalty payments due and owing to Consolidated Artists hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: TPC agrees that it shall keep general financial records. Consolidated Artists and Advantage, at Consolidated Artists' expense, shall have the right during the Contract Period and until two (2) years after the termination of this Agreement to inspect and make copies of the books and records of TPC.
perturbation.explanation: The deletion of the phrase *showing all Endorsed Products manufactured, distributed and sold insofar as they relate to the computation of royalty payments due and owing to Consolidated Artists hereunder* from Section 13 creates a contradiction with sections defining royalty payments, particularly Sections 11 and 12. With this removal, there is no assurance or procedure that ensures correct calculation of Royalty Compensation, contradicting the intent of Royalty Compensation, making royalty payment ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. ROYALTY COMPENSATION FOR ENDORSED PRODUCT. In addition to the Retainer Fee, Tournament Bonuses and Money List Bonuses set forth in Paragraphs 8, 9 and 10 above, TPC agrees to pay Consolidated Artists on behalf of Ogle royalty compensation of ten percent (10%) of the net sales of all Endorsed Products distributed or sold during the Contract Period in or to the Countries of Australia and New Zealand ("Royalty Compensation"). "Net sales" shall mean the gross invoice price billed to customers, less customary trade/quantity discounts, rebates and returns actually credited, but with no deductions of any kind. No costs incurred by TPC in the manufacture, advertisement, promotion or exploitation of any Endorsed Product shall be deducted as a cost in calculating the net sales. TPC agrees to guarantee to Consolidated Artists on behalf of Ogle a minimum Royalty Compensation of Twenty Thousand U.S. Dollars ($20,000) payable in four (4) equal installments of Five Thousand U.S. Dollars ($5,000) and due simultaneously with the Retainer Fee on or before the first (1st) day of February, May, August and November in each Contract Year.
perturbation.justification: The original text required TPC to maintain specific records related to 'Endorsed Products manufactured, distributed and sold' for accurate royalty calculation. The changed text weakens this requirement to 'general financial records,' removing the specificity. This omission directly contradicts Section 11, which mandates a 10% royalty compensation based on the 'net sales of all Endorsed Products distributed or sold.' Without detailed records of Endorsed Products as previously required, the 'net sales' calculation becomes ambiguous and unverifiable, thus impeding the royalty compensation scheme defined in Section 11.
